channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:

  3. NASA says meteorite landed in Cape Cod Bay  Yahoo
  4. Meteor over Massachusetts causes explosion reports, sightings from Delaware to Montreal  CNN
  5. Meteor explodes over Massachusetts, setting off loud booms | Nasa  The Guardian

item.description:

  4. The Blue Moon rises tonight: Where and when to see the second full moon of May  Space
  5. Clear skies give Metro Detroit perfect Blue Moon viewing weather  ClickOnDetroit | WDIV Local 4

item.title: We couldn't afford to pay for my mom's dementia assisted living anymore. She moved into a tiny house next door to me. - Business Insider
item.description: We couldn't afford to pay for my mom's dementia assisted living anymore. She moved into a tiny house next door to me.  Business Insider

item.title: The Sun is not standing still. It is carrying the entire Solar System around the centre of the Milky Way, and one lap takes roughly 230 million years. The last time we were this far around the galaxy, Earth was in the Triassic Period and the very first dinosaurs wer - Space Daily
item.description: The Sun is not standing still. It is carrying the entire Solar System around the centre of the Milky Way, and one lap takes roughly 230 million years. The last time we were this far around the galaxy, Earth was in the Triassic Period and the very first dinosaurs wer  Space Daily

item.title: 'Astonishing': James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'Astonishing': James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe  Live Science
  2. The Galaxy That Forgot to Spin  Universe Today
  3. The James Webb Telescope photographed a gigantic galaxy less than 2 billion years after the Big Bang that simply does not rotate, a behavior astronomers only expected to see in much older galaxies.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.title: There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way. A major global estimate put the planet’s tree count at about three trillion, while NASA gives the Milky Way’s star count as roughly 100 to 400 billion. - Space Daily
item.description: There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way. A major global estimate put the planet’s tree count at about three trillion, while NASA gives the Milky Way’s star count as roughly 100 to 400 billion.  Space Daily

  4. The Race That Keeps Changing Shape: Human Minds and Artificial Intelligence  The Times of Israel
  5. Lab-Grown Brain Organoids Propel Advances in Biocomputer Technology  Bioengineer.org

item.title: Scientists Intrigued by Chunk of Flesh That Refuses to Die After Several Years - Futurism
item.description:
1. Scientists Intrigued by Chunk of Flesh That Refuses to Die After Several Years  Futurism
  2. A severed piece of sea cucumber refused to die, and what happened next could transform medicine  Phys.org
  3. Amputated sea cucumber parts show signs of ‘immortality,’ researchers say  CNN
  4. Severed sea cucumber appendages don’t seem to die  Ars Technica
  5. Sea cucumber tissue heals itself and survives for years after being detached from the body  Earth.com

item.title: Watch this bio-inspired Mars rover concept 'swim' through sand on curved wheels (video) - Space

item.title: When Zhang Chenxing, who holds a PhD from MIT, co-founded Mega Engine Technology in Xi'an in early 2024, China's high-pressure oxygen-rich staged-combustion know-how sat almost entirely inside state propulsion houses — and by May 2026 his startu - Space Daily
item.description:
1. When Zhang Chenxing, who holds a PhD from MIT, co-founded Mega Engine Technology in Xi'an in early 2024, China's high-pressure oxygen-rich staged-combustion know-how sat almost entirely inside state propulsion houses — and by May 2026 his startu  Space Daily
  2. Chinese startup Mega Engine advances reusable staged-combustion rocket engine  SpaceNews
  3. China successfully fires reusable engine to compete with SpaceX  Interesting Engineering
  4. Chinese Startup Fires ‘Blazing’ Oxygen-Rich Engine for Deep Space Missions  Gizmodo

item.title: The Moon is stealing time from the Earth, and it has been getting away with it for billions of years. Our planet spins so much slower than it once did that a single day has stretched from just 19 hours to the 24 we live by, and the Moon is still creeping away from u - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The Moon is stealing time from the Earth, and it has been getting away with it for billions of years. Our planet spins so much slower than it once did that a single day has stretched from just 19 hours to the 24 we live by, and the Moon is still creeping away from u  Space Daily
  2. A Day on Earth Is Getting Longer as the Planet’s Rotation Slows  WSJ
  3. Goodbye to the 24-hour day: from this date onwards, days on Earth will last 25 hours  ECOticias.com
  4. Are we running out of 24-hour days? Climate change is slightly stretching time  Business Today
  5. Something ‘unprecedented’ is now happening to Earth’s rotation, scientists say  BBC Science Focus Magazine

item.title: Reported ancient mammoth bones found deep in Alaska were actually whales from 250 miles away - Yahoo
item.description: Reported ancient mammoth bones found deep in Alaska were actually whales from 250 miles away  YahooSee more headlines & perspectives on Google News

item.title: The bootprints left by the Apollo astronauts will still be sitting on the Moon a million years from now, because there is no wind and no rain to wear them away. - Space Daily
item.description: The bootprints left by the Apollo astronauts will still be sitting on the Moon a million years from now, because there is no wind and no rain to wear them away.  Space Daily

item.title: A single bolt of lightning can heat the air around it to roughly five times the temperature of the surface of the Sun, and it is the violent expansion of that superheated air that creates the crack of thunder. - Space Daily
item.description: A single bolt of lightning can heat the air around it to roughly five times the temperature of the surface of the Sun, and it is the violent expansion of that superheated air that creates the crack of thunder.  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists find hundreds of inherited DNA patterns that defy classic Mendelian genetics - The Brighter Side of News
item.description: Scientists find hundreds of inherited DNA patterns that defy classic Mendelian genetics  The Brighter Side of News

item.title: There are more atoms in a single glass of water than there are glasses of water in all the world's oceans combined — and if you marked every atom in one glass, dumped it into the sea, and waited for the oceans to mix completely, every glass of water on Earth - Space Daily
item.description: There are more atoms in a single glass of water than there are glasses of water in all the world's oceans combined — and if you marked every atom in one glass, dumped it into the sea, and waited for the oceans to mix completely, every glass of water on Earth  Space Daily

item.description: Evidence of cosmic-ray acceleration from a nearby supernova remnant  Phys.org

item.title: Strange Event Might Have Been A Primordial Black Hole Winking At Us - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Strange Event Might Have Been A Primordial Black Hole Winking At Us  ScienceAlert
  2. Something Just Passed Between Us and a Distant Star.  Universe Today
  3. An Unknown Object Passed Between Earth And A Star In The Large Magellanic Cloud, In An Event That Lasted An Hour  IFLScience
  4. A glow lasting only 1 hour observed in space in 2019 may have revealed a primordial black hole with a mass of 3 moons, associated with dark matter and considered 100,000 times more likely than common stellar objects by scientists in the study.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. A Flash in the Sky Reveals a Possible Object That Passed for an Hour Between Our Galaxy and the Large Magellanic Cloud  La Brújula Verde

item.title: Millions of Bees Have Thrived Under a New York Cemetery for More Than a Century - WIRED
item.description:
1. Millions of Bees Have Thrived Under a New York Cemetery for More Than a Century  WIRED
  2. A NY cemetery has been hiding 5.5 million bees underground for a century  The Weather Network
  3. Scientists find 5.5 million bees hiding underground in New York | Bees may have lived there for over 100 years | Inshorts  Inshorts
  4. A New York Cemetery Was Hiding Over 5 Million Burrowing Bees, One of the World's Largest Concentrations  Good News Network
  5. A New York cemetery was hiding 5.5 million bees underground  ScienceDaily

item.title: The oxygen in Earth's atmosphere is essential to almost every animal alive today — but when it first started accumulating in the air roughly 2.4 billion years ago, it triggered the most lethal pollution event in the planet's history, wiping out the vast majority of spec - Space Daily
item.description: The oxygen in Earth's atmosphere is essential to almost every animal alive today — but when it first started accumulating in the air roughly 2.4 billion years ago, it triggered the most lethal pollution event in the planet's history, wiping out the vast majority of spec  Space Daily

item.title: The Sun might look yellow, but seen from space without an atmosphere filtering its light, the sun is actually white — and the yellow color we see from Earth is the result of our atmosphere scattering blue wavelengths away, in the same physics that makes the sk - Space Daily
item.description: The Sun might look yellow, but seen from space without an atmosphere filtering its light, the sun is actually white — and the yellow color we see from Earth is the result of our atmosphere scattering blue wavelengths away, in the same physics that makes the sk  Space Daily

item.title: Countries at risk if ‘Doomsday Glacier’ size of Britain collapses as scientists warn when it could happen - LADbible
item.description:
1. Countries at risk if ‘Doomsday Glacier’ size of Britain collapses as scientists warn when it could happen  LADbible
  2. 'Poised to disintegrate': Antarctica's 'Doomsday Glacier' is set to lose its ice shelf this year  Live Science
  3. Ice shelf the size of Delhi to detach from Doomsday Glacier this year | Thwaites is largest glacier in West Antarctica | Inshorts  Inshorts
  4. Scientists issue stark warning as 'Doomsday Glacier’ size of Britain could collapse this year  LADbible
  5. A glacier bigger than Washington state is melting faster than ever. The implications are dire  OregonLive.com

item.title: The Sun Just Let Out The Longest Radio Burst We've Ever Seen - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. The Sun Just Let Out The Longest Radio Burst We've Ever Seen  ScienceAlert

  3. The Sun Just Did Something Nobody Expected and it Kept Going For 19 Days  Universe Today
  4. Strange 19-Day Solar Signal Breaks NASA’s Previous Record Nearly Fourfold  The Debrief
  5. Record-breaking solar radio burst lasted 19 days. NASA and ESA probes reveal what happened  WION
